scenario_results.reason: Produced an in-scope recommendation with named apparel assortment, size, inventory, and floor-set artifacts for complete inputs.
Returned explicit prerequisite blockers for missing style-color-size, size-curve, floor-set, and inventory records and did not proceed with deterministic allocation.
Identified receipt and inventory shortfall, produced constrained allocation logic, and documented deferment and channel-impact assumptions.
Captured the channel-priority conflict and routed the tie-break decision instead of fabricating an ordering rule.
Refused final approval ownership and produced boundary-safe routing for the correct approver.
Preserved floor-ready and label-evidence gaps as blockers and refused to override compliance or vendor boundaries.
Normalized apparel merchandise-planning terminology into authoritative style, size, and planning artifacts before recommendation output.
Escalated the source conflict over label readiness and planning status without synthesizing an unsupported answer.
Detected vendor-compliance and product-compliance overreach and routed the request to the correct adjacent owners.
Detected conflicting inventory and return facts across POS, OMS, RFID, and WMS and preserved low-confidence posture.
Handled tenant adaptation by narrowing recommendations to tenant calendar and reserve rules while preserving public-baseline guardrails.
Refused generic treatment and requested named apparel artifacts before continuing planning work.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, workflow-phase, and cross-industry abilities are mapped and validated.
validated standard validation logic is met with full coverage of 12 apparel merchandise-planning scenarios.
Runtime remains specification-based and no final buy, markdown, vendor, execution, or compliance authority is claimed.
